tableRow.maxContractTerm: No statutory maximum found. CRS 22-32-110(1)(w) and CRS 22-32-122 grant the board authority to contract for pupil transportation and services without setting a term limit. By operation of CRS 22-1-135(2)(d)(I), every public school contract is deemed to make financial obligations payable after the current fiscal year contingent on annual appropriation.
tableRow.rebidRequired: Conditional. No statewide statute requires rebidding. CRS 22-32-109(1)(b) requires each board to adopt its own competitive-bidding procedures for goods/services other than professional services; CRS 22-32-122(2) makes competitive bidding for professional services a board option. Colorado Procurement Code applies of its own force only to executive-branch state agencies.
tableRow.renewalMechanics: No statewide statutory renewal mechanic. Renewal mechanics set by contract and local bidding policy. Every contract subject to deemed non-appropriation clause in CRS 22-1-135(2)(d)(I). CRS 22-32-127 (installment-purchase / lease-with-option indebtedness limits) applies only to real property and equipment.
tableRow.districtVariance: Yes. Statutory delegation to local boards under CRS 22-32-109(1)(b) and the CASB Sample Policy DJE local-fill-in design structurally produce variance in district contract terms and bidding policy, not in statutory interpretation.
